title: AI / Machine Learning Engineer
emergingNote: AI adoption in South Africa is accelerating across banking (fraud detection, credit scoring), insurance (claims automation), healthcare (diagnostic AI), mining (predictive maintenance), and retail (demand forecasting). Standard Bank, Nedbank, Discovery, and Vodacom have dedicated AI teams. The NDP (National Development Plan) identifies AI as strategic for SA's economic competitiveness. CSIR and academic institutions are building AI research capacity.
overview: AI and Machine Learning engineers design, build, and deploy intelligent systems that learn from data. In South Africa, this field is emerging rapidly with applications in banking fraud detection, agricultural disease identification, healthcare diagnostics, and natural language processing for SA's 11 official languages.
dayInTheLife: An ML engineer at a Cape Town AI startup spends the morning fine-tuning a large language model on South African legal text, adapting it to understand SA legal terminology for a contract analysis product. Testing the model against held-out data reveals it misinterprets certain Afrikaans legal terms, triggering a data collection task to improve training examples. A weekly ML team meeting reviews the performance roadmap. Afternoon involves deploying an improved fraud detection model to the production environment at a fintech client — coordinating with DevOps for a zero-downtime deployment and monitoring the first 4 hours of live inference for anomalies.

qualifications.name: BSc Hons / MSc in AI or Machine Learning

marketGap.reason: South Africa has fewer than 500 qualified ML engineers against rapidly growing demand from financial services, healthcare, and agriculture. Remote work has enabled some SA ML engineers to earn international salaries, further depleting the local pool.

skills: Python and deep learning frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow)
Cloud ML platforms (AWS SageMaker, Azure ML)
